original_abstract: Many scientific disciplines have achieved major breakthroughs by adopting online platforms that organize, structure, and analyze data at a level of detail that traditional journals simply cannot accommodate. In this paper, we introduce OpenML: an open-science platform designed for the machine learning community to share and organize data, code, and experiments in fine detail. By moving beyond summarized PDF reports to a structured, networked commons, OpenML allows researchers to work more efficiently, increase their professional visibility, and collaborate globally to tackle complex challenges. We explore how OpenML fits into the broader landscape of networked science and outline the specific advantages it offers to researchers, students, and practitioners alike.
humanized_abstract: Several scientific fields have made tremendous progress with the aid of online resources that enable efficient processing of large amounts of data in much greater detail than it is possible in scientific journals. This work presents OpenML, an open science platform for the machine learning community that facilitates the sharing and management of data, code and experimental results in much greater detail than possible in a simple PDF paper. By shifting from the summary nature of PDF papers to a more structured format on a community-driven data commons, OpenML enables scientists to work more efficiently, gain more visibility and collaborate more efficiently on the world's most complex problems. We discuss OpenML in the context of network science and illustrate its advantages for scientists, students and practitioners.
